title: Small-town fight over corruption, abuse may haunt GOP candidate for Arizona governor
description: Rep. Andy Biggs, the party’s likely nominee, faced criticism from fellow Republicans over his handling of a small police force more than a decade ago.

title: Republicans don’t want to go there again on Trump’s 2020 election fraud claims
description: The president pressed the GOP to embrace his latest allegations, but many candidates kept their focus on issues they see as more salient to voters.

title: With just a week left until August recess, Congress faces long to-do list
description: Before the House adjourns for its August recess, House Speaker Mike Johnson wants to advance more defense funding, the president's election integrity bill and a stock trading ban for lawmakers.

title: Republican strategist on the hurdles the GOP faces to pass reconciliation bill
description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Republican strategist Rob Burgess about Republican efforts to move forward with a $95 billion party-line budget reconciliation bill.

title: Maine Democrats close in on pick to replace Senate candidate Graham Platner
description: Maine Democrats appear to be settling on a frontrunner to replace former Senate candidate Graham Platner in a rushed process to take on Republican Sen. Susan Collins.

title: Georgia official discusses Trump's claims about election security
description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks with Gabriel Sterling, of the Georgia Secretary of State's Office, about President Trump's claims about election security.

title: Voters are struggling to keep up with Iran war’s twists and turns
description: Americans have grown frustrated with President Donald Trump over his handling of the war. Here’s what several said about how they’re processing the constant changes.

title: Trump said the election system is broken. What do swing voters make of the claim?
description: What did some swing state voters think of President Trump's primetime speech on elections?

title: $600,000 for a presidential pardon? Inside the growing industry of clemency lobbying
description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks with NOTUS reporter Jasmine Wright about the growing lobbying industry to get presidential pardons under the second Trump administration.

title: Politics chat: How much faith can you have in the country's election systems?
description: President Trump and Secretary of Homeland Security Markwayne Mullin make claims about the vulnerability of election systems in the U.S., but how true are those claims?

title: Cuban artist and dissident exiled from country after 5 years in prison arrives in US
description: A famous Cuban dissident artist and musician, Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, arrived in Miami on Saturday after being released from a five-year prison sentence on the condition that he leave his country.

title: Week in Politics: Trump's speech; midterm elections; Todd Blanche and Jay Clayton
description: Three months from the November midterms, President Trump is drawing attention to issues that are not top priorities for voters.

title: As competitive Senate map emerges, both Democrats and Republicans see a path to win
description: More than halfway through the primary elections, the map of competitive Senate races is becoming clearer, though there are still outstanding races to watch.
